question: Which queries have the most disk spill?
Show me the performance red flags — slow queries, disk spill, failures.
What is the cache hit rate by warehouse?
What is the query volume breakdown by source type?
How has Genie space usage changed week over week in the last 3 months?
What percentage of queries come from Genie spaces vs dashboards vs notebooks?
Which users consume the most total query duration?
Which warehouses have the highest average query duration?
What is the daily trend of query counts and average duration in the last 7 days?
How has the failure rate changed over the last 30 days?
Compare query volume from Tableau vs Power BI vs dbt.
What is the P95 query duration by warehouse this week?
Which users run the most queries? Show top 10.

description: ID of the account.
Statement id of query that inserted result in cache when result is fetched from cache, otherwise store the query's statement id.
Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
The name of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
The ID of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
The ID of the user who ran the statement.
Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.

The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
TRUE indicates that the statement result was fetched from the cache.
Total number of rows returned by the statement.
The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
The number of bytes of files pruned after table partition and file pruning.
Query parameters values associated with the query. Only one of named_parameters or pos_parameters can be populated.

A struct that contains key-value pairs representing one or more Databricks entities that were involved in the execution of this statement, such as jobs, notebooks, or dashboards. This field only records Databricks entities and are not sorted by execution order. Statement executions that contain multiple IDs indicate that the execution was triggered by multiple entities: for example, an Alert may trigger on a Job result and call a SQL Query, so all three IDs will be populated within query_source.
The user-supplied custom tags associated with this statement execution.
The number of bytes of files read after IO pruning.
The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.
The number of bytes of files read after table partition and file pruning.
The percentage of bytes of persistent data read from the IO cache.
The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
The number of rows output from scans after table partition, file, in file and row pruning.
Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
The Spark session ID.
The total amount of data in bytes sent over the network.
Size of data, in bytes, temporarily written to disk while executing the statement.
The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).

The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
The size in bytes of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
The number of files of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
Total number of rows of persistent data written to cloud object storage.

Metric view with pre-defined measures (Total Queries, Avg Duration, P50/P95/P99 latency, Cache Hit Rate, Failure Rate, Spill Rate, data volume) and dimensions (time, user, warehouse, query source, performance flags). Use MEASURE() to reference measures.

content:
=== DATA SOURCES ===
1. system.query.history — raw query history with structs (compute, query_source) and byte/ms units.
2. ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv — METRIC VIEW with pre-defined measures and dimensions.

=== WHEN TO USE EACH SOURCE ===
METRIC VIEW: Use for aggregate/KPI questions — totals, averages, rates, percentiles, counts by dimension.
  Syntax: SELECT dim, MEASURE(`measure_name`) FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv WHERE ... GROUP BY ALL
  Filter on `Query Date` (date type) for the metric view, not `Start Time`.
RAW TABLE: Use when user needs statement_text, error_message, query_tags, individual query details, or columns not in the metric view.
  Filter on start_time (timestamp) for the raw table.

=== TIME FILTERING (REQUIRED) ===
Always apply a time filter. Default to last 7 days if not specified.
  Metric view: `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  Raw table: start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS

=== RAW TABLE TIPS ===
Structs: compute.warehouse_id, compute.type, query_source.notebook_id, query_source.dashboard_id, query_source.job_info.job_id (nested!), query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id (nested!).
Convert bytes to GB: divide by 1024*1024*1024. Convert ms to seconds: divide by 1000.
Use try_divide() to avoid division by zero. Truncate SQL: LEFT(statement_text, 200).
execution_status: FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED. Filter to FINISHED for perf analysis.
Cost proxy: total_duration_ms — longer duration = more DBU consumption.
For detail queries (individual slow/spill/failed queries), always include statement_id so users can look up the query in Query History.

=== METRIC VIEW MEASURES ===
Volume: Total Queries, Unique Users, Warehouses Used
Duration: Avg/Max/P50/P95/P99 Duration Sec, Total Duration Sec
Wait: Total Compute Wait Sec, Total Capacity Wait Sec, Avg Wait Pct
Data: Total Read GB/TB, Total Written/Shuffle/Spill GB
Flags: Slow/Very Slow/Spill/Failed/Cached/Long Wait/Poor Scan Query Count
Rates: Cache Hit Rate Pct, Failure Rate Pct, Spill Rate Pct

When asked about data read in TB, use MEASURE(`Total Read TB`). When asked about data read in GB, use MEASURE(`Total Read GB`). Do NOT use Total Queries for data volume questions.

=== METRIC VIEW DIMENSIONS ===
Time: Start Time, Query Date, Query Hour, Day of Week
Identity: Executed By, Warehouse ID, Workspace ID
Classification: Statement Type, Execution Status, Query Source Type, Client Application
Flags: Is Slow Query, Has Disk Spill, Is Cached, Is Tableau, Is Power BI, Is dbt
IDs: Notebook/Dashboard/Job/Alert/Genie Space/Pipeline/SQL Query ID

=== QUERY SOURCE vs CLIENT APPLICATION ===
Query Source Type = which Databricks entity triggered the query (Notebook, Dashboard, Job, etc.).
Client Application = which external tool connected (Tableau, Power BI, dbt).
These are different dimensions — use the right one based on the question.

question: Duration breakdown — where is time spent for the slowest queries?
sql:
SELECT executed_by,
  LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms / 1000 AS compute_wait_sec,
  waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms / 1000 AS queue_wait_sec,
  compilation_duration_ms / 1000 AS compile_sec,
  execution_duration_ms / 1000 AS exec_sec,
  result_fetch_duration_ms / 1000 AS fetch_sec,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS total_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
ORDER BY total_duration_ms DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Which Genie spaces generate the most queries?
sql:
SELECT query_source.genie_space_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.genie_space_id
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Who is writing the most data?
sql:
SELECT executed_by,
  COUNT(*) AS write_queries,
  SUM(written_bytes) / (1024*1024*1024) AS written_gb,
  SUM(written_rows) AS total_rows_written
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND written_bytes > 0
GROUP BY executed_by
ORDER BY written_gb DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show me queries that are both slow and have disk spill
sql:
SELECT statement_id,
  executed_by,
  LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec,
  try_divide(spilled_local_bytes, 1024*1024*1024) AS spilled_gb,
  start_time
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND total_duration_ms > 60000
  AND spilled_local_bytes > 0
ORDER BY duration_sec DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Which dashboards drive the most queries?
sql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(query_source.dashboard_id, query_source.legacy_dashboard_id) AS dashboard_id,
  CASE WHEN query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'AI/BI' ELSE 'Legacy' END AS dashboard_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_viewers,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND (query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL OR query_source.legacy_dashboard_id IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY dashboard_id, dashboard_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: What is the cache hit rate and spill rate by warehouse?
sql:
SELECT `Warehouse ID`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries,
  MEASURE(`Cache Hit Rate Pct`) AS cache_hit_rate_pct,
  MEASURE(`Spill Rate Pct`) AS spill_rate_pct
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Warehouse ID` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY total_queries DESC

question: What is the P95 query duration by warehouse this week?
sql:
SELECT `Warehouse ID`,
  MEASURE(`P95 Duration Sec`) AS p95_duration_sec
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND `Warehouse ID` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY p95_duration_sec DESC

question: Which queries read the most data?
sql:
SELECT statement_id,
  executed_by,
  LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  read_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS read_gb,
  read_rows,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
ORDER BY read_bytes DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Which queries are waiting the longest for compute?
sql:
SELECT executed_by,
  compute.warehouse_id,
  LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms / 1000 AS compute_wait_sec,
  waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms / 1000 AS capacity_wait_sec,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS total_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND (waiting_for_compute_duration_ms > 30000 OR waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms > 30000)
ORDER BY waiting_for_compute_duration_ms + waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms DESC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the query volume by source type in the last 7 days?
sql:
SELECT `Query Source Type`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries,
  MEASURE(`Unique Users`) AS unique_users
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY total_queries DESC

question: Compare query volume across BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, dbt)
sql:
SELECT client_application,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  try_divide(SUM(read_bytes), (1024*1024*1024)) AS total_read_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND (client_application ILIKE '%Tableau%' OR client_application ILIKE '%Power BI%' OR client_application ILIKE '%dbt%')
GROUP BY client_application
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: What is the failure rate trend over the last 30 days?
sql:
SELECT `Query Date`,
  MEASURE(`Failure Rate Pct`) AS failure_rate_pct
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY `Query Date`

question: Find duplicate or repeated queries
sql:
SELECT LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  COUNT(*) AS times_run,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_sec,
  SUM(CASE WHEN from_result_cache THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS cache_hits
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
  AND statement_type = 'SELECT'
GROUP BY LEFT(statement_text, 200)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY times_run DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Show the performance red flags summary for the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries,
  MEASURE(`Slow Query Count`) AS slow_queries,
  MEASURE(`Very Slow Query Count`) AS very_slow_queries,
  MEASURE(`Spill Query Count`) AS spill_queries,
  MEASURE(`Cached Query Count`) AS cached_queries,
  MEASURE(`Failed Query Count`) AS failed_queries,
  MEASURE(`Long Wait Query Count`) AS long_wait_queries,
  MEASURE(`Poor Scan Query Count`) AS poor_scan_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL

question: What is the total data read in GB in the last 7 days?
sql:
SELECT MEASURE(`Total Read GB`) AS total_read_gb
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
system.query.history.client_application ILIKE '%Tableau%'
system.query.history.client_application ILIKE '%dbt%'
system.query.history.client_application ILIKE '%Power BI%'
system.query.history.query_source.notebook_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.query_source.job_info.job_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.query_source.alert_id IS NOT NULL
system.query.history.execution_status = 'FAILED'
system.query.history.total_duration_ms > 60000
system.query.history.spilled_local_bytes > 0
system.query.history.from_result_cache = true

question: What is the cache hit rate by warehouse?

content:
SELECT `Warehouse ID`,
  MEASURE(`Cache Hit Rate Pct`) AS cache_hit_rate_pct
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Warehouse ID` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY cache_hit_rate_pct DESC

question: Which queries have the most disk spill in the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT statement_id,
  executed_by,
  LEFT(statement_text, 200) AS query_preview,
  try_divide(spilled_local_bytes, 1024*1024*1024) AS spilled_gb,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec,
  start_time
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND spilled_local_bytes > 0
ORDER BY spilled_gb DESC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the daily query count trend over the last 30 days?

content:
SELECT `Query Date`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY `Query Date`

question: Which users run the most queries in the last 7 days? Show the top 10.

content:
SELECT `Executed By`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS query_count
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: What is the P95 query duration by warehouse in the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT `Warehouse ID`,
  MEASURE(`P95 Duration Sec`) AS p95_duration_sec
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Warehouse ID` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY p95_duration_sec DESC

question: How has Genie space query volume changed week over week in the last 3 months?

content:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', start_time) AS week_start,
  COUNT(*) AS genie_query_count
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 90 DAYS
  AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY week_start
ORDER BY week_start

question: Show the performance red flags summary for the last 7 days

content:
SELECT MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries,
  MEASURE(`Slow Query Count`) AS slow_queries,
  MEASURE(`Spill Query Count`) AS spill_queries,
  MEASURE(`Failed Query Count`) AS failed_queries,
  MEASURE(`Long Wait Query Count`) AS long_wait_queries,
  MEASURE(`Poor Scan Query Count`) AS poor_scan_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL

question: How many queries ran yesterday?

content:
SELECT MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` = current_date() - INTERVAL 1 DAY
GROUP BY ALL

question: What is the total data read in GB in the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT MEASURE(`Total Read GB`) AS total_read_gb
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL

question: Which Genie spaces have the most unique users in the last 30 days?

content:
SELECT query_source.genie_space_id,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
  AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.genie_space_id
ORDER BY unique_users DESC

question: What is the hourly query volume pattern for the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT `Query Hour`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY `Query Hour`

question: What is the daily failure rate trend over the last 30 days?

content:
SELECT `Query Date`,
  MEASURE(`Failure Rate Pct`) AS failure_rate_pct
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY `Query Date`

question: What is the query count from Genie spaces vs dashboards vs notebooks?

content:
SELECT `Query Source Type`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Query Source Type` IN ('Genie Space', 'AI/BI Dashboard', 'Notebook')
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY total_queries DESC

question: What is the query volume by source type in the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT `Query Source Type`,
  MEASURE(`Total Queries`) AS total_queries,
  MEASURE(`Unique Users`) AS unique_users
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY total_queries DESC

question: What is the average wait time as a percentage of total duration by warehouse?

content:
SELECT `Warehouse ID`,
  MEASURE(`Avg Wait Pct`) AS avg_wait_pct
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Warehouse ID` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY avg_wait_pct DESC

question: Which users consume the most total query duration (cost proxy) in the last 7 days?

content:
SELECT `Executed By`,
  MEASURE(`Total Duration Sec`) AS total_duration_sec
FROM ryant_catalog.default.query_history_mv
WHERE `Query Date` >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND `Executed By` IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY total_duration_sec DESC
